title: Thelocephala riparia
taxonomy: Thelocephala riparia is a species of liverwort, a non-vascular plant that belongs to the division Marchantiophyta. It is a type of bryophyte, which is a group of small, non-vascular plants that includes mosses, liverworts, and hornworts. The division Marchantiophyta is a subgroup of the larger plant kingdom and is characterized by the absence of vascular tissue. Thelocephala riparia is further classified as belonging to the class Marchantiopsida, which includes other liverwort species. This classification is based on the plant's morphological characteristics, such as its leaf structure and reproductive features.

intro: Thelocarpa riparia, also known as Thelocarpa or Thelocarpa riparia, is a type of plant that belongs to the family Apocynaceae, commonly referred to as a type of thorny vine.
